purpose: Define a bounded offline replay and adjudication trial for generic specialist process wording without changing live owner selection.
why_this_surface: It has the cleanest allowlisted family set among the currently instrumented unresolved surfaces.
It does not sit directly on the payment-dispute edge.
The telemetry already shows bounded disagreement examples without requiring complaint-family reopening.

prefer_runtime_owner_when: coverage wording is present and runtime winner is eligibility_status_coverage_root_v1
destination or arrangement wording is present and runtime winner is routing_specialist_referral_confusion_v1
candidate_override_to_referral_status_wh: the message asks whether a referral is needed or whether a visit is possible without referral
shadow family is service_referral_status_root_v1 with a margin that would exceed the future live threshold
bounded_offline_rule.fallback: keep runtime winner on weak or mixed evidence

proposed_future_live_guardrails: Only arbitrate among allowlisted existing owners
Low confidence keeps the runtime winner
Any free-care wording keeps eligibility authoritative
Any destination or arrangement wording keeps routing authoritative unless replay data proves otherwise
offline_replay_plan: Collect a larger shadow sample for generic_specialist_process_residue
Bucket rows into referral-needed, routing/arrangement, and free-care subshapes
Measure false-positive risk against stable routing and eligibility neighbors
Only consider a live pilot if replay shows clear net improvement with no protected-neighbor theft
not_allowed: No owner override in production yet
No answer synthesis from shadow predictions
No expansion beyond the allowlisted family set
No delegation to broad free reasoning
